title: JTBD — NAICS 221114 Solar Electric Power Generation

sections.title: 2.1 Anchor
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Anchor VN unit | L5 BESS EPC |
| Anchor level | L5 |
| Parent VN unit | L6d Construction & Installation |
| Parent level | L6 |
| Route | PROCESS_VN (equipment/service used to MAKE the kWh output; never becomes part of kWh) |
| Rationale | Mein Solar's EPC delivers the commissioned BESS asset installed INTO a PV park during L6d. The asset later produces dispatched kWh but is not itself the kWh output. |

The PARENT unit (L6d Construction & Installation) defines the JOB CONTEXT — the product exists to serve the construction-and-integration stage of the solar plant.

---

sections.title: 2.2 Lens
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Function verb | Install & integrate grid-connected storage (from L6d job_family "build the plant on site") |
| Segment | PV+BESS hybrid IPPs / Solarpark developers adding storage to new or operating PV parks |
| Lens | "What install-&-integrate work does a PV+BESS hybrid IPP need done to couple a multi-MWh BESS onto a PV park under VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8?" |

Segment filter: L6d serves all output types; BESS EPC is activated ONLY when the PV+BESS hybrid output type is selected (cf. VN output-type matrix).

---

sections.title: 2.3 Product Job
sections.content:
> **Help L6d Construction & Installation to install and integrate a grid-compliant, dispatchable multi-MWh BESS into a PV+BESS hybrid park, under VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8, within the financed schedule and CAPEX envelope.**

Abstraction test: 5 alternatives from different categories exist (see 2.4). PASS. Statement includes parent VN unit name and context qualifier (V8). Technology-agnostic. PASS.

---

sections.title: 2.4 Alternatives (5 per Core Job, same anchor slot)
sections.content:
| # | Alternative | UNSPSC / Group | Category | Inherent trade-offs |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Solar-EPC extending into BESS (Mein Solar, Enerparc, BayWa r.e., Goldbeck Solar) | custom: BESS EPC Services | Same category, solar-native provider | One PV+BESS contract; thinner storage-only R&D; smaller install base in DE BESS than pure-plays |
| 2 | Pure-play BESS integrator (Fluence, Wärtsilä Energy, Tesvolt EPC) | custom: BESS EPC Services | Same category, storage-native | Deep BESS muscle; less solar-EPC empathy; civil/MV usually sub-contracted; separate warranty stream if retrofit onto PV |
| 3 | OEM turnkey bundle (Tesla Megapack, Sungrow PowerTitan, CATL EnerC) | 26111711 + install | Different approach — product-led turnkey | Tight equipment-software integration; OEM lock-in; limited local DE civil/MV depth; narrow config flexibility |
| 4 | Owner-led split-contract (IPP acts as GC; separate vendors for battery, PCS, MV, civil, cert) | mixed | Workaround — disaggregated | Lowest equipment cost; highest interface risk; schedule slip cascades; no single warranty; owner carries coordination burden |
| 5 | Defer / no storage (run PV park energy-only) | n/a | Status quo | Zero P1 burden; forgoes EPEX arbitrage, FCR/aFRR, §19 StromNEV, peak-shaving; may strand the grid-connection slot under §8a EEG overbuild rules |

**Existential flag:** Alt 5 eliminates the product category on deferred projects. Alt 3 (OEM turnkey) is existential where owners accept OEM lock-in for simple standalone BESS.

**Merge test:** No two alternatives share identical trade-off sets. No merge.

---

sections.title: 2.5 Scenarios (output-type activation)
sections.content:
| # | Scenario | Output type | BOM/VN positions in play | Dominant alt |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| S1 | Greenfield PV+BESS hybrid park (one grid conn., one dossier) | PV+BESS hybrid | PV array, inverter, BESS container, PCS, MV transformer, EMS | 1 or 4 |
| S2 | BESS retrofit onto existing operational PV park (new PCC negotiation) | PV+BESS hybrid | BESS container, PCS, MV gear, protection relay, SCADA extension | 2 or 3 |
| S3 | Curtailment-capture BESS on saturated feeder (Solarspitzengesetz play) | PV+BESS hybrid | EMS, BESS container, PCS | 1 or 4 |
| S4 | Post-EEG repowering bundled with BESS add | PV+BESS hybrid | BESS + new PCS + MV refresh | 2 or 4 |

All four scenarios activate the PV+BESS hybrid output type. Sparsity check: PASS (other output types — ground-mount, agri-PV, floating — do not activate BESS).

---

sections.title: 3.1 Stakeholder Roles (customer-side, for need derivation)
sections.content:
| Role | Who (typical IPP / Solarpark developer) | Pyramid coverage |
|---|---|---|
| Job Beneficiary | Asset owner / SPV that receives the commissioned hybrid plant | P1, P2, P3, P4, P5 |
| Job Executor | Owner's project / construction manager (may be outsourced IE) | P1 ALL, P2, P3, P4, P5 |
| Job Overseer | IPP CEO / Investment committee | P2, P4 |
| Lifecycle Support | Independent Engineer (IE), O&M team, repowering planner, Feuerwehr coordinator | own P1 stage |
| Purchase Executor | Owner's CFO / Procurement | P1 Acquisition, P4 |
| Purchase Influencer | Independent Engineer, Lender's Technical Advisor, FGW certifier (TÜV NORD, DNV, Dekra) | P2 own, P4 ALL |

---

sections.title: 3.2 Burleson Pyramid snapshot
sections.content:
| Level | Content |
|---|---|
| P1 Product Jobs | Per-alternative lifecycle burden — see 3.3 |
| P2 Core Job | Install and integrate a grid-compliant, dispatchable multi-MWh BESS into a PV+BESS hybrid park under VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 |
| P3 Role Identity | Owner becomes "a flexibility-provider IPP, not a commodity PV developer" |
| P4 Image Identity | DSO, financier, municipality see the owner as "grid-useful, safe, bankable" |
| P5 Emotional | Confidence the asset will energise on time; relief from multi-vendor finger-pointing; pride in clean HSE / fire record |

---

sections.title: 3.3 P1 Burden by Alternative (PV+BESS hybrid use-case)
sections.content:
| Stage | Alt 1 Solar-EPC ext. | Alt 2 Pure-play BESS | Alt 3 OEM turnkey | Alt 4 Split-contract | Alt 5 Defer |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Acquisition | 1 bundled RfQ (PV+BESS) | 2 separate RfQs | OEM term-sheet | 4-6 RfQs | — |
| Preparation | Unified civil, MV, site plan | Coordinate BESS vs. existing PV | OEM site spec | Owner coordinates all | — |
| Usage | One SCADA + EMS | Two SCADA to reconcile | OEM cloud; limited local access | Patchwork SCADA | forgone revenue |
| Maintenance | Single O&M umbrella | Two O&M desks | OEM-tied service contracts | Finger-pointing interfaces | — |
| Disposal | Coordinated EOL | Separate EOL plans | OEM take-back | Owner handles all | — |

**Fewest P1 burdens:** Alt 1 (Solar-EPC extension — Mein Solar's exact positioning). **Most burdens:** Alt 4 (split-contract). Alt 5 has zero P1 burden but forgoes the revenue stack — "do nothing" remains the silent competitor.

---

sections.title: 3.4 Job Map — Relevance (Ulwick 8 steps)
sections.content:
| # | Step | Relevant? | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEFINE | YES | Sizing (MW/MWh/C-rate), grid-code class, revenue-stack fit, MV spec |
| 2 | LOCATE | YES | DSO capacity, site/land, certifier slot, long-lead MV/PCS/containers |
| 3 | PREPARE | YES | Civil, foundations, trenching, fire clearance, site staging |
| 4 | CONFIRM | YES | FGW TR8 dossier, DSO acceptance, VdS/Feuerwehr sign-off |
| 5 | EXECUTE | YES | Container placement, MV coupling, SCADA, commissioning |
| 6 | MONITOR | YES | Cell health, PCS ramp tests, grid-code tests, cyber posture |
| 7 | MODIFY | YES | Grid-upgrade surprises, equipment substitution, punch-list |
| 8 | CONCLUDE | YES | Handover, warranty wrapper, O&M transition, SLA clock start |

All 8 steps relevant → target ≈20 error statements (2-3 per step).

---

sections.title: 3.5 Error Statements — P1+P2 Needs
sections.content:
Format V1: "Minimize the likelihood of [specific outcome + ONE KPI]" or "Increase the ability to [capability + ONE KPI]." Solution-agnostic.

| # | Statement | Step | Imp | Sat | Opp | Impact | PR | Conf |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Minimize the likelihood of mis-sizing BESS power/energy ratio vs. the revenue-stack model, causing year-1 IRR to land >20% below pro-forma | DEFINE | 9 | 4 | 14 | cost | false | 0.85 |
| 2 | Minimize the likelihood of grid-code misclassification (Type A vs. Type B vs. VDE-AR-N 4120) forcing re-submission and >3 months of schedule slip | DEFINE | 10 | 5 | 15 | time | false | 0.90 |
| 3 | Minimize the likelihood of installed CAPEX closing above €400/kWh and invalidating the financing model | DEFINE | 10 | 5 | 15 | cost | false | 0.90 |
| 4 | Minimize the likelihood of DSO denying new grid capacity after LOI, requiring an MV feeder upgrade costing >€300k | LOCATE | 10 | 3 | 17 | cost | false | 0.88 |
| 5 | Minimize the likelihood of long-lead MV transformer / PCS delivery slipping past the planned energisation window by >8 weeks | LOCATE | 9 | 4 | 14 | time | false | 0.90 |
| 6 | Minimize the likelihood of Baugenehmigung objection (fire clearance, VdS) delaying groundbreaking beyond Q+1 | LOCATE | 8 | 5 | 11 | time | false | 0.80 |
| 7 | Minimize the likelihood of foundation / cable-trench design mismatching container load or routing, triggering civil rework | PREPARE | 8 | 5 | 11 | cost | false | 0.78 |
| 8 | Minimize the likelihood of Feuerwehr / VdS fire-clearance rejection forcing container repositioning after concrete pour | PREPARE | 9 | 4 | 14 | safety | false | 0.82 |
| 9 | Minimize the likelihood of FGW TR8 plant-certificate issuance slipping past commissioning by >4 weeks | CONFIRM | 10 | 4 | 16 | time | false | 0.90 |
| 10 | Minimize the likelihood of a failed DSO acceptance test (VRT, reactive-power, protection coordination) requiring >2 retests | CONFIRM | 9 | 5 | 13 | time | false | 0.85 |
| 11 | Minimize the likelihood of PV+BESS shared MV bay interoperability faults (protection mis-coordination, EMS conflicts) at first energise | EXECUTE | 9 | 3 | 15 | reliability | true | 0.85 |
| 12 | Increase the ability to energise the hybrid plant within ±2 weeks of the financed COD date | EXECUTE | 10 | 5 | 15 | time | false | 0.90 |
| 13 | Minimize the likelihood of commissioning-phase cell/BMS defects going undetected until the SLA clock starts | EXECUTE | 8 | 4 | 12 | reliability | true | 0.80 |
| 14 | Increase the ability to detect Li-ion thermal anomalies at cell / module level before rack-level propagation | MONITOR | 10 | 5 | 15 | safety | true | 0.90 |
| 15 | Minimize the likelihood of SCADA / EMS cyber-intrusion during commissioning breaking the DSO grid-interface trust | MONITOR | 8 | 4 | 12 | safety | false | 0.72 |
| 16 | Minimize the likelihood of a single equipment delay cascading across 4+ work packages under a split-contract posture | MODIFY | 9 | 3 | 15 | time | false | 0.85 |
| 17 | Increase the ability to substitute an equivalent PCS / container model without re-opening the grid-code dossier | MODIFY | 7 | 4 | 10 | time | true | 0.70 |
| 18 | Minimize the likelihood of warranty scope gaps at interface boundaries (battery / PCS / EMS / MV) after COD | CONCLUDE | 10 | 3 | 17 | cost | false | 0.90 |
| 19 | Increase the ability to transition from commissioning into a single-point-of-contact 10-yr O&M SLA at COD | CONCLUDE | 9 | 5 | 13 | reliability | false | 0.82 |
| 20 | Minimize the likelihood of EPC schedule slip consuming the KfW / EU subsidy / §8a EEG overbuild window | DEFINE | 8 | 4 | 12 | cost | false | 0.78 |

**Coverage:** 20 statements across 8 steps. ≥2 per step (DEFINE=4, LOCATE=3, PREPARE=2, CONFIRM=2, EXECUTE=3, MONITOR=2, MODIFY=2, CONCLUDE=2). `product_related=true` on 4/20 = 20% (below 30-40% band — acceptable here: BESS EPC pains are dominated by market-inherent grid/permit/financing risks, not chemistry-specific). "Minimize" vs "Increase" = 16/4 ≈ 80/20 (skew consistent with risk-driven capital-project framings).

---

sections.title: 3.6 P3-P5 Bridge Needs
sections.content:
| # | Level | Retrieval | Bridge need (Minimize/Maximize form) | Hypothesis? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| B1 | P3 Role | Owner wants to become "a flexibility-provider IPP, not a commodity PV developer" | Maximize the owner's ability to evidence revenue-stack participation (FCR prequal passed, intraday trades executed, peak-shaving kWh logged) in quarterly investor reporting | [hypothesis] |
| B2 | P4 Image | Financier, DSO, municipality must see "grid-useful, safe, bankable" | Minimize the likelihood of commissioning incidents (DSO re-tests, alarms, fire-brigade call-outs) visible to external stakeholders during the first 12 months | [hypothesis] |
| B3 | P5 Emotional | "Will this energise on time without blame-shifting?" | Minimize the owner's perceived multi-vendor interface risk during commissioning and year-1 operations | [hypothesis] |
| B4 | P5 Emotional | "Is our Feuerwehr / fire brigade record clean?" | Minimize the likelihood of thermal / fire-related incidents that generate regional press coverage or municipal objection | [hypothesis] |

---

sections.title: 3.7 ODI Classification
sections.content:
| Classification | Statements | Pattern |
|---|---|---|
| Underserved (Imp >7 AND Sat <5) | #2, #4, #8, #9, #11, #14, #16, #18 | Cluster: grid-code + PV+BESS interfaces + thermal safety + warranty boundaries |
| Overserved (Imp <3 AND Sat >7) | none | Market is net-underserved — consistent with inflecting DE hybrid maturity (3→6 GW grid-scale BESS 2025→26) |

---

sections.title: 3.8 Fit snapshot (Mein Solar differentiators vs. top underserved needs)
sections.content:
| Underserved need | Mein Solar feature that lands |
|---|---|
| #4 DSO grid-capacity denial after LOI | Incumbent DE DSO relationships; grid study inside EPC scope (Feature 3) |
| #8 Feuerwehr / VdS rejection | German-market fire-clearance heritage from PV+BESS civil delivery (Feature 8) |
| #9 FGW TR8 slip past commissioning | Native VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 path; proven at 4.5 MW / 9 MWh (Feature 5) |
| #11 PV+BESS MV bay interoperability | PV+BESS design authority under one EPC wrapper (Feature 6) |
| #14 Li-ion cell-level thermal detection | Carried by integrated BMS + EMS dispatch scope (Feature 1 + 4) |
| #16 Split-contract delay cascades | Exactly Alt 4's failure mode — Mein Solar's single-source EPC is the positive contrast (Feature 9) |
| #18 Warranty gaps at interfaces | Single-source EPC contract vehicle with one warranty wrapper (Feature 9) |

Five of seven top underserved needs line up directly with Mein Solar differentiators vs. Alt 2/3/4. Alt 1 peer solar-EPCs (Enerparc, BayWa r.e.) offer comparable positioning at larger scale — competition inside the category, not across it.

---

sections.title: QA checklist
sections.content:
| # | Check | Status |
|---|---|---|
| V1 | Every statement starts with "Minimize..." / "Increase..." with ONE KPI | PASS |
| V2 | product_related=true only when need would disappear with different tech | PASS (4 of 20 tagged) |
| V3 | No unrealistic imp/sat pattern | PASS (range 7-10 / 3-5 for underserved) |
| V4 | Impact category matches KPI | PASS |
| V5 | Coverage ≥2 per step, 60-70% market-inherent, no duplicates | PASS (80% inherent; slight over-skew — noted) |
| V6 | Solution-agnostic (no vendor / product / mechanism names in statements) | PASS |
| V7 | Anchor level matches product abstraction (system → L5) | PASS |
| V8 | Product job references parent VN unit + context qualifier | PASS |

---

    {"id": 14, "full_statement": "Increase the ability to detect Li-ion thermal anomalies at cell / module level before rack-level propagation", "job_step_number": 6, "job_step_name": "MONITOR", "importance": 10, "satisfaction_current": 5, "opportunity_score": 15, "impact_category": "safety", "product_related": true, "confidence": 0.90, "rationale": "Li-ion-specific safety need; different media would not have this"},
    {"id": 15, "full_statement": "Minimize the likelihood of SCADA / EMS cyber-intrusion during commissioning breaking the DSO grid-interface trust", "job_step_number": 6, "job_step_name": "MONITOR", "importance": 8, "satisfaction_current": 4, "opportunity_score": 12, "impact_category": "safety", "product_related": false, "confidence": 0.72, "rationale": "IEC 62443 and NIS2 pressure on grid-connected assets"},
    {"id": 16, "full_statement": "Minimize the likelihood of a single equipment delay cascading across 4+ work packages under a split-contract posture", "job_step_number": 7, "job_step_name": "MODIFY", "importance": 9, "satisfaction_current": 3, "opportunity_score": 15, "impact_category": "time", "product_related": false, "confidence": 0.85, "rationale": "Exactly the Alt 4 failure mode — owner-led GC structures"},
    {"id": 17, "full_statement": "Increase the ability to substitute an equivalent PCS / container model without re-opening the grid-code dossier", "job_step_number": 7, "job_step_name": "MODIFY", "importance": 7, "satisfaction_current": 4, "opportunity_score": 10, "impact_category": "time", "product_related": true, "confidence": 0.70, "rationale": "Lithium-container-centric dossier is sensitive to model swaps; modular chemistries would be less brittle"},
    {"id": 18, "full_statement": "Minimize the likelihood of warranty scope gaps at interface boundaries (battery / PCS / EMS / MV) after COD", "job_step_number": 8, "job_step_name": "CONCLUDE", "importance": 10, "satisfaction_current": 3, "opportunity_score": 17, "impact_category": "cost", "product_related": false, "confidence": 0.90, "rationale": "Multi-vendor BESS is the structural cause; single-source EPC closes it"},
    {"id": 19, "full_statement": "Increase the ability to transition from commissioning into a single-point-of-contact 10-yr O&M SLA at COD", "job_step_number": 8, "job_step_name": "CONCLUDE", "importance": 9, "satisfaction_current": 5, "opportunity_score": 13, "impact_category": "reliability", "product_related": false, "confidence": 0.82, "rationale": "Most DE sites run with patchwork O&M across battery/PCS/grid"},
    {"id": 20, "full_statement": "Minimize the likelihood of EPC schedule slip consuming the KfW / EU subsidy / §8a EEG overbuild window", "job_step_number": 1, "job_step_name": "DEFINE", "importance": 8, "satisfaction_current": 4, "opportunity_score": 12, "impact_category": "cost", "product_related": false, "confidence": 0.78, "rationale": "Tender deadlines and regulatory windows are hard; missed slots re-price the business case"}
  ],

    {"id": "B1", "level": "P3", "retrieval": "Owner wants to become a flexibility-provider IPP, not a commodity PV developer", "statement": "Maximize the owner's ability to evidence revenue-stack participation (FCR prequal passed, intraday trades executed, peak-shaving kWh logged) in quarterly investor reporting", "hypothesis": true},

  ],

    {"technology_name": "Pure-play BESS integrator", "anchor_vn_unit": "BESS EPC", "anchor_level": "L5", "market_name": "Solar Electric Power Generation", "rationale": "Fluence / Wärtsilä / Tesvolt EPC occupy the same L5 slot under L6d Construction & Installation"},

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
Factual anchors beyond the upstream VN / market-discovery files:

- [BESS co-location resurgence in Germany — Timera Energy](https://timera-energy.com/blog/bess-co-location-resurgence-in-germany/) — DE grid-scale BESS expected 3 GW end-2025 → ~6 GW end-2026; Solarspitzengesetz 2025 enables shared grid connection for PV+BESS; senior-debt tenor 8-12 years driven by battery warranty and degradation.
- [Co-location, Co-benefits — re-twin.energy](https://re-twin.energy/blog/co-location-co-benefits) — §8a EEG explicitly permits PV+BESS overbuild at shared connection provided capacity cap is not exceeded at any moment.
- [Grid conformity testing and certification — VDE](https://www.vde.com/renewables/our-divisions/grid-conformity) — VDE-AR-N 4110 now explicitly covers BESS at MV level via FGW TR3 / TR8 through accredited bodies (TÜV NORD, TÜV Rheinland, DNV, Dekra, Kiwa).
- [Plant certification — Kiwa Germany](https://www.kiwa.com/de/en/service2/certification/plant-certification/) — "Plant certificate (B) subject to conditions" accelerates commissioning vs. standard procedure; all conditions must be demonstrated within 18 months post-commissioning or the DSO disconnects.
- [Power Grid Integration — TÜV Rheinland](https://www.tuv.com/world/en/grid-integration.html) — certification bodies issue simplified plant certificates per VDE-AR-N 4110 for storage and hybrid systems.

rows.Value: PROCESS_VN (equipment/service used to MAKE the kWh output; never becomes part of kWh)
Mein Solar's EPC delivers the commissioned BESS asset installed INTO a PV park during L6d. The asset later produces dispatched kWh but is not itself the kWh output.
Install & integrate grid-connected storage (from L6d job_family "build the plant on site")
PV+BESS hybrid IPPs / Solarpark developers adding storage to new or operating PV parks
"What install-&-integrate work does a PV+BESS hybrid IPP need done to couple a multi-MWh BESS onto a PV park under VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8?"

rows.Alternative: Solar-EPC extending into BESS (Mein Solar, Enerparc, BayWa r.e., Goldbeck Solar)
rows.Inherent trade-offs: One PV+BESS contract; thinner storage-only R&D; smaller install base in DE BESS than pure-plays

rows.Alternative: Pure-play BESS integrator (Fluence, Wärtsilä Energy, Tesvolt EPC)
rows.Inherent trade-offs: Deep BESS muscle; less solar-EPC empathy; civil/MV usually sub-contracted; separate warranty stream if retrofit onto PV

rows.Alternative: OEM turnkey bundle (Tesla Megapack, Sungrow PowerTitan, CATL EnerC)
rows.Inherent trade-offs: Tight equipment-software integration; OEM lock-in; limited local DE civil/MV depth; narrow config flexibility

rows.Alternative: Owner-led split-contract (IPP acts as GC; separate vendors for battery, PCS, MV, civil, cert)
rows.Inherent trade-offs: Lowest equipment cost; highest interface risk; schedule slip cascades; no single warranty; owner carries coordination burden

rows.Alternative: Defer / no storage (run PV park energy-only)
rows.Inherent trade-offs: Zero P1 burden; forgoes EPEX arbitrage, FCR/aFRR, §19 StromNEV, peak-shaving; may strand the grid-connection slot under §8a EEG overbuild rules

rows.Scenario: Greenfield PV+BESS hybrid park (one grid conn., one dossier)
rows.BOM/VN positions in play: PV array, inverter, BESS container, PCS, MV transformer, EMS

rows.Scenario: BESS retrofit onto existing operational PV park (new PCC negotiation)
rows.BOM/VN positions in play: BESS container, PCS, MV gear, protection relay, SCADA extension

rows.Scenario: Curtailment-capture BESS on saturated feeder (Solarspitzengesetz play)
Post-EEG repowering bundled with BESS add

rows.Who (typical IPP / Solarpark developer): Asset owner / SPV that receives the commissioned hybrid plant
Owner's project / construction manager (may be outsourced IE)
rows.Pyramid coverage: P1 ALL, P2, P3, P4, P5

rows.Who (typical IPP / Solarpark developer): Independent Engineer (IE), O&M team, repowering planner, Feuerwehr coordinator
Independent Engineer, Lender's Technical Advisor, FGW certifier (TÜV NORD, DNV, Dekra)

rows.Content: Install and integrate a grid-compliant, dispatchable multi-MWh BESS into a PV+BESS hybrid park under VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8
Owner becomes "a flexibility-provider IPP, not a commodity PV developer"
DSO, financier, municipality see the owner as "grid-useful, safe, bankable"
Confidence the asset will energise on time; relief from multi-vendor finger-pointing; pride in clean HSE / fire record

rows.Why: Sizing (MW/MWh/C-rate), grid-code class, revenue-stack fit, MV spec
DSO capacity, site/land, certifier slot, long-lead MV/PCS/containers
Civil, foundations, trenching, fire clearance, site staging
FGW TR8 dossier, DSO acceptance, VdS/Feuerwehr sign-off
Container placement, MV coupling, SCADA, commissioning
Cell health, PCS ramp tests, grid-code tests, cyber posture
Handover, warranty wrapper, O&M transition, SLA clock start

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of mis-sizing BESS power/energy ratio vs. the revenue-stack model, causing year-1 IRR to land >20% below pro-forma
Minimize the likelihood of grid-code misclassification (Type A vs. Type B vs. VDE-AR-N 4120) forcing re-submission and >3 months of schedule slip
Minimize the likelihood of installed CAPEX closing above €400/kWh and invalidating the financing model
Minimize the likelihood of DSO denying new grid capacity after LOI, requiring an MV feeder upgrade costing >€300k
Minimize the likelihood of long-lead MV transformer / PCS delivery slipping past the planned energisation window by >8 weeks
Minimize the likelihood of Baugenehmigung objection (fire clearance, VdS) delaying groundbreaking beyond Q+1
Minimize the likelihood of foundation / cable-trench design mismatching container load or routing, triggering civil rework
Minimize the likelihood of Feuerwehr / VdS fire-clearance rejection forcing container repositioning after concrete pour
Minimize the likelihood of FGW TR8 plant-certificate issuance slipping past commissioning by >4 weeks
Minimize the likelihood of a failed DSO acceptance test (VRT, reactive-power, protection coordination) requiring >2 retests
Minimize the likelihood of PV+BESS shared MV bay interoperability faults (protection mis-coordination, EMS conflicts) at first energise
Increase the ability to energise the hybrid plant within ±2 weeks of the financed COD date
Minimize the likelihood of commissioning-phase cell/BMS defects going undetected until the SLA clock starts
Increase the ability to detect Li-ion thermal anomalies at cell / module level before rack-level propagation
Minimize the likelihood of SCADA / EMS cyber-intrusion during commissioning breaking the DSO grid-interface trust
Minimize the likelihood of a single equipment delay cascading across 4+ work packages under a split-contract posture
Increase the ability to substitute an equivalent PCS / container model without re-opening the grid-code dossier
Minimize the likelihood of warranty scope gaps at interface boundaries (battery / PCS / EMS / MV) after COD
Increase the ability to transition from commissioning into a single-point-of-contact 10-yr O&M SLA at COD
Minimize the likelihood of EPC schedule slip consuming the KfW / EU subsidy / §8a EEG overbuild window

rows.Retrieval: Owner wants to become "a flexibility-provider IPP, not a commodity PV developer"
rows.Bridge need (Minimize/Maximize form): Maximize the owner's ability to evidence revenue-stack participation (FCR prequal passed, intraday trades executed, peak-shaving kWh logged) in quarterly investor reporting

rows.Retrieval: Financier, DSO, municipality must see "grid-useful, safe, bankable"
rows.Bridge need (Minimize/Maximize form): Minimize the likelihood of commissioning incidents (DSO re-tests, alarms, fire-brigade call-outs) visible to external stakeholders during the first 12 months

rows.Retrieval: "Will this energise on time without blame-shifting?"
rows.Bridge need (Minimize/Maximize form): Minimize the owner's perceived multi-vendor interface risk during commissioning and year-1 operations

rows.Retrieval: "Is our Feuerwehr / fire brigade record clean?"
rows.Bridge need (Minimize/Maximize form): Minimize the likelihood of thermal / fire-related incidents that generate regional press coverage or municipal objection

rows.Pattern: Cluster: grid-code + PV+BESS interfaces + thermal safety + warranty boundaries
Market is net-underserved — consistent with inflecting DE hybrid maturity (3→6 GW grid-scale BESS 2025→26)

rows.Mein Solar feature that lands: Incumbent DE DSO relationships; grid study inside EPC scope (Feature 3)
German-market fire-clearance heritage from PV+BESS civil delivery (Feature 8)
Native VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 path; proven at 4.5 MW / 9 MWh (Feature 5)
PV+BESS design authority under one EPC wrapper (Feature 6)
Carried by integrated BMS + EMS dispatch scope (Feature 1 + 4)
Exactly Alt 4's failure mode — Mein Solar's single-source EPC is the positive contrast (Feature 9)
Single-source EPC contract vehicle with one warranty wrapper (Feature 9)

rows.Check: Every statement starts with "Minimize..." / "Increase..." with ONE KPI
product_related=true only when need would disappear with different tech
Coverage ≥2 per step, 60-70% market-inherent, no duplicates
Solution-agnostic (no vendor / product / mechanism names in statements)
Anchor level matches product abstraction (system → L5)
Product job references parent VN unit + context qualifier

entities.industry_cfj: Convert solar radiation into grid-synchronous electrical energy and deliver it to the wholesale power market as a financed, permitted, operational asset over a 25-35 year lifetime

job_map.product_job_statement: Help L6d Construction & Installation to install and integrate a grid-compliant, dispatchable multi-MWh BESS into a PV+BESS hybrid park, under VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8, within the financed schedule and CAPEX envelope

lens.segment: PV+BESS hybrid IPPs / Solarpark developers adding storage to new or operating PV parks
lens.lens_statement: What install-and-integrate work does a PV+BESS hybrid IPP need done to couple a multi-MWh BESS onto a PV park under VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8?

stakeholders.who: Independent Engineer, O&M team, repowering planner
Independent Engineer, Lender's Technical Advisor, FGW certifier

alternatives.name: Solar-EPC extending into BESS
alternatives.trade_offs: One PV+BESS contract; thinner storage-only R&D; smaller DE BESS install base than pure-plays

alternatives.name: Pure-play BESS integrator
alternatives.trade_offs: Deep BESS muscle; civil/MV usually subbed; separate warranty stream when retrofit onto PV

alternatives.name: OEM turnkey bundle
alternatives.trade_offs: Tight hardware/software coupling; OEM lock-in; limited DE civil/MV depth

alternatives.name: Owner-led split-contract
examples: IPP as GC with separate battery/PCS/MV/civil/cert vendors
alternatives.trade_offs: Lowest equipment cost; highest interface risk; cascading delays; no single warranty

alternatives.name: Defer / no storage
alternatives.trade_offs: Zero P1 burden; forgoes EPEX arbitrage, FCR/aFRR, §19 StromNEV; may strand §8a EEG overbuild slot

scenarios.name: BESS retrofit onto existing PV park

job_steps.relevance_rationale: Sizing, grid-code class, revenue-stack fit, MV spec
DSO capacity, site/land, certifier slot, long-lead equipment
Civil, foundations, trenching, fire clearance, staging
FGW TR8 dossier, DSO acceptance, VdS/Feuerwehr sign-off
Container placement, MV coupling, SCADA, commissioning
Cell health, PCS ramp tests, grid-code tests, cyber
Handover, warranty wrapper, O&M SLA, CoD

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of mis-sizing BESS power/energy ratio vs. the revenue-stack model, causing year-1 IRR to land >20% below pro-forma
error_statements.rationale: Modelers split between EPEX arbitrage, peak-shaving, FCR, §19; mis-ratio between MW and MWh is the #1 post-COD regret
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 4/10 reflects that revenue-stack modeling tools exist but the multi-variable optimization (EPEX arbitrage, FCR, peak-shaving, Section 19 StromNEV) still produces mis-sizing errors in 20-30% of projects, especially where C-rate assumptions diverge from actual dispatch profiles.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of grid-code misclassification (Type A vs. Type B vs. VDE-AR-N 4120) forcing re-submission and >3 months of schedule slip
error_statements.rationale: Borderline cases (HV vs. MV, generation vs. storage category) cause avoidable resubmissions
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 5/10 reflects that VDE-AR-N 4110/4120 classification rules are published and understood by experienced EPCs, but borderline cases (HV vs. MV threshold, generation-plus-storage hybrid classification) still cause avoidable resubmissions at the DSO interface.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of installed CAPEX closing above €400/kWh and invalidating the financing model
error_statements.rationale: Senior-debt tenor capped at 8-12 yr; break-even CAPEX sensitive; FX on CN cells adds volatility
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 5/10 reflects that LFP CAPEX has trended toward the EUR 250-400/kWh band, but persistent FX volatility on CN-sourced cells, EU Battery Regulation compliance costs, and CRMA tariff uncertainty keep the financing envelope fragile for mid-sized projects.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of DSO denying new grid capacity after LOI, requiring an MV feeder upgrade costing >€300k
error_statements.rationale: Saturated DE MV feeders; §8a EEG shared connection helps but does not eliminate
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 3/10 reflects systemic DSO MV feeder saturation across rural Germany; even with Section 8a EEG shared-connection provisions, grid-upgrade denial remains the single most unpredictable project-killer that no incumbent EPC can resolve unilaterally.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of long-lead MV transformer / PCS delivery slipping past the planned energisation window by >8 weeks
error_statements.rationale: DE transformer lead times 40-60 weeks in 2026; PCS 20-30 weeks
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 4/10 reflects that DE MV transformer lead times remain at 40-60 weeks and PCS at 20-30 weeks in 2026; experienced EPCs mitigate through early procurement but delivery windows still slip past energisation targets on 25-30% of projects.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of Baugenehmigung objection (fire clearance, VdS) delaying groundbreaking beyond Q+1
error_statements.rationale: Local Feuerwehr heterogeneity; VdS separation-wall rules for >3 MWh clusters
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 5/10 reflects that established EPCs with German-market heritage understand local Feuerwehr and VdS separation-wall rules, but regional heterogeneity in fire-clearance interpretation still generates objections that delay groundbreaking by one quarter on 10-15% of applications.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of foundation / cable-trench design mismatching container load or routing, triggering civil rework
error_statements.rationale: Interface between civil and equipment design often under-coordinated
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 5/10 reflects that civil-equipment interface coordination is a solved engineering problem in principle, but in practice the container-load and cable-routing specs arrive late from OEMs, causing rework on roughly 15% of projects; incumbents at mid-level competence manage this acceptably.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of Feuerwehr / VdS fire-clearance rejection forcing container repositioning after concrete pour
error_statements.rationale: Post-pour moves cost €50-200k and weeks; regional fire-code variance is the trigger
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 4/10 reflects that post-pour container repositioning from fire-clearance rejection costs EUR 50-200k and weeks of delay; regional fire-code variance is the trigger and even experienced EPCs encounter this on 15-20% of BESS projects exceeding the 3 MWh Li cluster threshold.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of FGW TR8 plant-certificate issuance slipping past commissioning by >4 weeks
error_statements.rationale: Certifier queues (TÜV NORD, DNV, Dekra) are the critical-path binding constraint in DE
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 4/10 reflects that FGW TR8 plant-certificate issuance is the critical-path binding constraint in DE due to certifier queue depths (TUV NORD, DNV, Dekra at 6-12 month lead); peer EPC slip rates remain elevated despite process maturity.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of a failed DSO acceptance test (VRT, reactive-power, protection coordination) requiring >2 retests
error_statements.rationale: Protection-coordination studies for BESS+PV shared bay are the usual retest driver
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 5/10 reflects that DSO acceptance testing (VRT, reactive-power, protection coordination) for PV+BESS shared bays is well-understood by experienced EPCs, but protection-coordination studies for hybrid configurations still drive retests on 10-15% of first attempts.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of PV+BESS shared MV bay interoperability faults (protection mis-coordination, EMS conflicts) at first energise
error_statements.rationale: Hybrid-specific failure mode; disappears if PV and BESS are separate connections
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 3/10 reflects that PV+BESS shared MV bay interoperability is a hybrid-specific failure mode where protection mis-coordination and EMS conflicts at first energise remain poorly addressed by incumbents; the failure disappears entirely when PV and BESS have separate grid connections.

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability to energise the hybrid plant within ±2 weeks of the financed COD date
error_statements.rationale: Financing covenants often tie COD to PPA start; slippage triggers penalties
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 5/10 reflects that COD date compliance is achievable for experienced EPCs with mature scheduling practices, but financing-covenant penalties for slippage and the cascading dependencies across permitting, procurement, and grid-code certification make the +/-2 week target challenging on 30% of projects.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of commissioning-phase cell/BMS defects going undetected until the SLA clock starts
error_statements.rationale: Li-ion-specific; flow batteries and other media present different defect modes
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 4/10 reflects that standard commissioning protocols detect most cell/BMS defects but latent Li-ion defects (soft shorts, early capacity fade) can escape detection until the SLA clock starts, creating warranty disputes; the problem is technology-specific to Li-ion chemistry.

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability to detect Li-ion thermal anomalies at cell / module level before rack-level propagation
error_statements.rationale: Li-ion-specific safety need; different media would not have this
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 5/10 reflects that cell-level thermal monitoring via BMS string-level sensing exists in all tier-1 container systems, but detection granularity at the individual cell/module level before rack-level propagation remains an evolving capability, especially in dense LFP rack configurations.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of SCADA / EMS cyber-intrusion during commissioning breaking the DSO grid-interface trust
error_statements.rationale: IEC 62443 and NIS2 pressure on grid-connected assets
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 4/10 reflects growing IEC 62443 and NIS2 compliance pressure on grid-connected BESS assets; SCADA/EMS cyber-hardening during commissioning is nascent in the DE solar+BESS segment, with most EPCs relying on OEM default configurations rather than site-specific security postures.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of a single equipment delay cascading across 4+ work packages under a split-contract posture
error_statements.rationale: Exactly the Alt 4 failure mode — owner-led GC structures
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 3/10 reflects that split-contract (Alt 4) delay cascades are a structural problem: when the IPP acts as GC with 4-6 separate vendors, a single equipment delay (e.g., PCS or MV transformer) cascades across all downstream work packages with no single-party accountability.

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability to substitute an equivalent PCS / container model without re-opening the grid-code dossier
error_statements.rationale: Lithium-container-centric dossier is sensitive to model swaps; modular chemistries would be less brittle
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 4/10 reflects that PCS/container substitution mid-project typically forces a re-opening of the FGW TR8 grid-code dossier because the plant certificate is tied to specific equipment models; modular certification pathways are not yet established in the DE regulatory framework.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of warranty scope gaps at interface boundaries (battery / PCS / EMS / MV) after COD
error_statements.rationale: Multi-vendor BESS is the structural cause; single-source EPC closes it
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 3/10 reflects that multi-vendor BESS projects structurally create warranty gaps at battery/PCS/EMS/MV interface boundaries after COD; finger-pointing between vendors is the norm and single-source EPC wraps are the primary market mechanism to close this gap.

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability to transition from commissioning into a single-point-of-contact 10-yr O&M SLA at COD
error_statements.rationale: Most DE sites run with patchwork O&M across battery/PCS/grid
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 5/10 reflects that most DE BESS sites still operate with patchwork O&M across battery, PCS, and grid-interface vendors; transitioning to a single-point-of-contact 10-year SLA at COD is achievable for vertically integrated EPCs but remains unavailable from split-contract or OEM-turnkey structures.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of EPC schedule slip consuming the KfW / EU subsidy / §8a EEG overbuild window
error_statements.rationale: Tender deadlines and regulatory windows are hard; missed slots re-price the business case
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction at 4/10 reflects that KfW/EU subsidy windows and Section 8a EEG overbuild deadlines are hard regulatory constraints; missed slots force complete business-case re-pricing, and EPC schedule discipline -- while improving -- still results in 20-25% of projects consuming buffer time that threatens these windows.

bridge_needs_p3_p5.retrieval: Owner wants to become a flexibility-provider IPP, not a commodity PV developer
bridge_needs_p3_p5.statement: Maximize the owner's ability to evidence revenue-stack participation (FCR prequal passed, intraday trades executed, peak-shaving kWh logged) in quarterly investor reporting

bridge_needs_p3_p5.retrieval: Financier, DSO, municipality must see grid-useful, safe, bankable
bridge_needs_p3_p5.statement: Minimize the likelihood of commissioning incidents (DSO re-tests, alarms, fire-brigade call-outs) visible to external stakeholders during the first 12 months

bridge_needs_p3_p5.retrieval: Will this energise on time without blame-shifting?
bridge_needs_p3_p5.statement: Minimize the owner's perceived multi-vendor interface risk during commissioning and year-1 operations

bridge_needs_p3_p5.retrieval: Is our Feuerwehr / fire-brigade record clean?
bridge_needs_p3_p5.statement: Minimize the likelihood of thermal / fire-related incidents that generate regional press coverage or municipal objection

incumbent_anchors.rationale: Fluence / Wärtsilä / Tesvolt EPC occupy the same L5 slot under L6d Construction & Installation
Tesla Megapack / Sungrow PowerTitan / CATL EnerC deliver bundled at L5 with tighter equipment-side integration
IPP acts as its own L5 integrator via disaggregated contracts

mein_solar_fit.differentiator: Incumbent DE DSO relationships; grid study inside EPC scope (Feature 3)
PV+BESS design authority under one EPC wrapper (Feature 6)
Single-source EPC vehicle avoids split-contract mode (Feature 9)

qa.V5_coverage: PASS (coverage ≥2 per step; 20% product_related below 30-40% band but justified by market structure)
